catalysts.summary: Caris announced that it will report second-quarter 2026 results on August 5, 2026 after the market close; the event is a dated validation milestone, not bullish evidence by itself.
catalysts.title: CAI scheduled Q2 2026 results for August 5

catalysts.summary: SEC Form 4 reports director Jon Halbert indirectly purchased 68,000 CAI shares on May 15, 2026 at a weighted-average price of $14.56.
catalysts.title: CAI director purchased 68,000 shares

criteria.rationale: Pass requires liabilities/assets at most 75% or current ratio at least 1.2; the looser 90%/1.0 boundary is partial.
Pass requires positive annual operating cash flow less reported capital expenditure.
Pass requires positive annual revenue growth; a decline no worse than 5% is partial.
Pass requires annual diluted-share growth at most 2%; up to 5% is partial.
No current source-attributed observation is available.
Pass requires positive annual net income without a decline greater than 20%; a larger positive-income decline is partial.

catalysts.summary: The Texas Attorney General announced an investigation into Celsius Holdings over representations and marketing practices involving energy drinks marketed to teens and children; treat the unresolved proceeding as a CELH invalidation risk, not proof of wrongdoing.
catalysts.title: Texas AG opened an investigation into Celsius marketing practices

catalysts.summary: SEC Form 4 reports Chairman and CEO John Fieldly purchased 8,475 CELH shares on May 22, 2026 at $29.36.
catalysts.title: CELH Chairman and CEO purchased 8,475 shares

catalysts.summary: SEC Form 4 reports President and COO Eric Hanson purchased 7,500 CELH shares on May 21, 2026 at $29.04.
catalysts.title: CELH President and COO purchased 7,500 shares

catalysts.summary: Celsius reported FY2025 basic EPS of $0.25 versus $0.46 and net income attributable to common stockholders of $63.8m versus $107.5m; annual gross margin was 50.4% versus 50.2%, while Q4 margin fell to 47.4% from 50.2%. Alani Nu retail sales rose 101%, core CELSIUS brand revenue rose 7.5% for the year, and Q4 core-brand revenue fell about 8% during the Pepsi distribution transition.
catalysts.title: CELH FY2025 growth was acquisition-heavy while attributable earnings declined
